query: Why have movie trailers not only become much longer but now most seem to include the entire plot and end scenes condensed into the trailer?

baseline.final_answer: Movie trailers have become longer and often reveal too much of the plot, including endings, which can detract from the viewing experience. This practice is criticized by viewers and theatre owners, though studios view trailers as valuable advertising space.

evaluation.feedback: The answer makes factual claims about movie trailers revealing plots and endings, but provides no citations to support these claims. This violates the grounding policy.
